---
title: "50by30 Microdata Pipeline"
---

This book documents the data pipeline used to **harmonize, upload, review, and distribute** microdata used to monitor the UNHCR **50by30 vision**: that 50 percent of refugees in protracted crises are self-reliant by 2035.

## The Pipeline at a Glance

```{mermaid}
%%| label: fig-pipeline
%%| fig-cap: "The 50by30 microdata pipeline, from raw survey to distribution."
flowchart LR
    raw["<b>1 · Raw data</b><br/>PRIMUS · type=raw"]
    harm["<b>2 · Harmonization</b><br/>map to 50by30 schema"]
    rev{"<b>3 · Review</b>"}
    dist[("Datalibweb<br/>distributed")]

    raw --> harm --> rev
    rev -->|approve| dist
    rev -->|reject| harm

    classDef step fill:#e8f0fe,stroke:#4285f4,color:#174ea6;
    classDef gate fill:#fef7e0,stroke:#f9ab00,color:#7f6003;
    classDef store fill:#e6f4ea,stroke:#34a853,color:#0d652d;
    class raw,harm step;
    class rev gate;
    class dist store;
```

The pipeline has three steps:

1. **[Raw data](chapters/01-raw-upload.qmd)** — raw survey microdata is uploaded
   to Datalibweb via **PRIMUS**, unless it is already available there.
2. **[Harmonization](chapters/02-harmonization.qmd)** — raw data is harmonized to
   the common 50by30 schema.
3. **[Upload, review & approval](chapters/03-review-approval.qmd)** — harmonized
   files are uploaded and reviewed; approved files are published and made available
   for distribution from Datalibweb.

## Core Harmonized Schema

Every harmonized file must conform to the [50by30 schema](chapters/04-schema.qmd).
The core variables are:

| Variable | Status | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `welfare` | **Mandatory** | Household welfare aggregate (consumption or income) |
| `welfare_self` | **Mandatory** | Self-reliance-adjusted welfare measure |
| `weight` | **Mandatory** | Sampling weight |
| `age` | Optional | Age of the individual, in years |
| `educat4` | Optional | Educational attainment, 4-category classification |

See the [Schema & Variable Dictionary](chapters/04-schema.qmd) for the full list,
value ranges, and labeling conventions.

::: {.bg-light-yellow .pa3 .br2 .f6}
**Status:** This is a living document under active development.
See the [contributor guide](wiki/contributing.md) before making changes.
:::
